title: Prevent ISR events from disappearing during main-loop dispatch
prompt:
The fixed-capacity SPSC event queue in `src/event_queue.c` can lose an event that arrives from its interrupt-style producer while the main-loop consumer is inside a dispatch callback. The interrupt push reports success and writes into an unused slot, but after the callback returns the newly queued event is no longer reported as pending and is never dispatched. Repair the stale queue accounting at its root by releasing the consumed reservation with `atomic_fetch_sub_explicit` using release ordering, so a producer increment cannot be overwritten.

Preserve the public API and the queue's execution model: there is exactly one interrupt-style producer and one main-loop consumer, and dispatch keeps the consumed slot reserved until its handler returns. Events must remain FIFO. Storage must remain the fixed array embedded in `struct event_queue`; do not allocate or increase its capacity. When the queue is full, `event_queue_push_from_isr` must continue to drop the newest event, leave all queued events untouched, and increment the dropped counter. An interrupt that arrives from a handler while a full queue is being dispatched therefore still observes a full queue and is dropped.

Keep the implementation lock-free on the supported target: the unsigned-int atomics are required to be always lock-free, and accepted-event publication and consumed-slot release must retain their acquire/release handoff. Do not add locks, interrupt masking, retry/spin loops, platform dependencies, or dynamic allocation. Make the focused correction in `src/event_queue.c`. Do not modify the protected header or tests, weaken the strict C17 compiler flags, or make the tests timing-dependent. Run the provided test command; it is offline and deterministic.

full_distill.primary_skill: repair interrupt-safe SPSC queue accounting without weakening fixed-capacity overflow, lock-free atomic, or acquire/release contracts
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the strict C17 build succeeds, but the deterministic runtime fixture exits 1 after a dispatch handler injects an accepted ISR event: the consumer stores a count derived before the callback and overwrites the producer's increment, so the event remains in storage but becomes unreachable
full_distill.success_contract: accepted ISR events survive a main-loop dispatch interleaving and are delivered FIFO; wraparound and bounded storage remain correct; full-queue pushes drop only the newest event and increment the counter; no allocation, locks, interrupt masking, spinning, or non-lock-free atomics are introduced
adversarial_factors: the race is reproduced synchronously by an ordinary dispatch callback, so passing cannot depend on host thread scheduling or sleeps
the interrupt push is confirmed successful before the test checks that its event remains pending and is dispatched next
a full-queue callback injection pins the existing reservation rule and drop-newest overflow policy
FIFO tests cross the physical end of the embedded slot array and verify recovery after overflow
protected source-contract checks retain always-lock-free unsigned-int atomics, strict warning flags, acquire/release handoffs, bounded storage, and the absence of allocation and lock primitives
